title: BCA Assay for protein quantification
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"pUfgS","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"IX9Dz","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"BQrJj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":124871,"guid":"419E5C32341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit","mol_weight":0,"sku":"23225","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/23225","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":95,"name":"Thermo Fisher Scientific","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":205233,"guid":"41AE8B09341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"96-Well Microplate Flat Bottom non-sterile Polystyrene Clear 10/Pack 100/Case","mol_weight":0,"sku":"655101","url":"https://shop.gbo.com/en/usa/products/bioscience/microplates/96-well-microplates/96-well-microplates-clear/655101.html","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":193,"name":"greiner bio-one","link":"https://www.gbo.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Use provided 0.2% BSA for creating a standard curve: Dilute amount necessary for the assay 1:2 using lysis buffer

Label tubes (1.5 mL Eppendorf) for standards. Final Conc ug/mLVolume 0.1% BSAVolume buffer Standard (ug) Volume 0.1% BSA (uL) Volume buffer(uL) 0 0 20 2 2 18 4 4 16 6 6 14 8 8 12 10 10 10 15 15 5 20 20 0
Add diluted BSA to standard tubes last, before adding working reagent.
To each sample and all standards, add 1 mL Working Reagent (comprised of [1 mL Reagent A + 20 uL Reagent B] x number of samples + 10% extra for error). Mix well.
Place tubes in 37° C incubator for 30 minutes to react.
Aliquot 200 uL from each tube to a well of a clear, 96-well plate. This can be done in triplicate if desired.
Read on BCA program on GloMax plate reader at λ = 562 nm

Use values from BSA standards to create standard curve. Can be done in Excel, Statmost, or GraphPrizm. Seeking R2 value as close to 1 as possible. Ensure all your samples are within the standard curve range. If not, need to be redone.
Use the resultant y=mx+b equation to quantify the amount of protein in each sample. Dividing by 3 will give you your protein concentration per uL.
Keeping all protein on ice throughout, use 3 uL/ sample + 17 uL Lysis Buffer for a total volume of 20 uL.
Add appropriate amount of Lysis Buffer to all tubes for the standard curve, following chart above.
creator: Karina L Conkrite
